reasoning:
The 360-day window preceding October 26, 2025 covers roughly November 1, 2024 to October 26, 2025. Crucially, this excludes the massive wave of fatalities from July-August 2024 (where ~1,400 people died). Therefore, the threshold of 31.167 reflects the "normal" level of baseline violence under the interim government (primarily driven by mob lynchings and intra-party clashes, which averaged ~26-30 per month according to HRSS data). Since the threshold does not contain huge outliers, the distribution is not severely right-skewed, meaning the probability of a random month exceeding the mean is much closer to 40-45% than the 22.5% general base rate. 

Furthermore, multiple sources report that Bangladesh's interim government plans to hold general elections in early 2026 (estimates range from February to April 2026). Pre-election periods in Bangladesh historically witness sharp escalations in organized political violence. As we move from late 2025 into January 2026, the 30-day fatality counts are increasingly likely to surpass the recent 31.167 average due to heightening election campaigns and rival clashes. Consequently, my probabilities start at 0.38 for the immediate window (which appears typical) and rise to 0.55 for the pre-election January 2026 window.
